regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 19, 2026: Conditions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos will be generally stable with light to moderate east-southeast trade winds 4 to 12 knots and mostly overcast skies developing over the next three days. Temperatures will rise from the low 70s this morning to highs near 90°F by day three, with minimal rain chances under 10 percent. Mariners should expect calm seas today but plan for slightly choppier conditions by midweek as winds increase to around 12 knots. Yacht and fishing captains should secure light gear and monitor overcast conditions near exposed anchorages in San Juan and St. Thomas; passengers should dress in layers and stay hydrated with the steady warming trend. No marine wind warnings or tropical weather systems are present at this time.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 19, 2026: Conditions across Cozumel and surrounding ports are stable with northerly winds 12 to 15 knots and temperatures in the low to mid-70s Fahrenheit. Expect mostly overcast skies today with no rain, shifting to partly cloudy tomorrow as winds veer slightly northeast and increase to 17 knots. By day three, light rain showers are possible, but no significant precipitation is expected. Mariners should note moderate northerly winds favoring open-water navigation but maintain caution near exposed anchorages in Grand Cayman and Roatan where wind-driven seaway may increase; small craft operators should remain vigilant but no advisories are in effect. Cruise and fishing captains can plan for smooth operations with minimal weather disruptions through midweek, while passengers should carry light rain gear for Thursday’s brief showers.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region ports will see light drizzle with moderate NNE winds today, averaging 6 to 14 knots through March 21. Expect temperatures near 70-76°F with rain chances diminishing from 53% to under 20% by day three. Small craft operators and yacht captains should remain cautious in Freeport and Bimini where persistent drizzle may reduce visibility and increase risk of slippery decks; maintain vigilant watch and secure loose gear. Cruise passengers should carry light rain gear ashore and anticipate occasional wet conditions, especially in Nassau and Turks & Caicos, but no significant marine warnings are in effect.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean conditions remain stable for March 19, 2026. Aruba reports east winds at 17 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero rain chance; expect steady easterly trade winds through the next three days with occasional overcast conditions but minimal precipitation. Captains should maintain vigilance for moderate chop near offshore anchorages at Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire due to consistent 14-18 knot easterly to east-southeasterly winds; fishing operations will benefit from stable seas but should avoid late afternoon periods when cloud cover peaks. Passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with light winds and comfortable temperatures near 80-82°F, but mariners should monitor for any developing tropical moisture or disturbances that could alter conditions.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions for March 19, 2026 show moderate northerly winds 16-19 knots with partly cloudy skies and increasing rain chances through Day 2, particularly around Grand Cayman where showers are expected with a 58-60% probability. Roatan, Belize City, Cozumel, and Costa Maya should anticipate similar wind speeds and scattered showers, with improved conditions by Day 3 as winds shift slightly northeast and rain chances drop near 35%. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor localized squalls and maintain caution when navigating nearshore or anchoring in exposed areas such as Grand Cayman’s West Bay, where gusty winds and showers may reduce visibility and increase seas. Passengers should plan for intermittent rain and variable winds, securing loose gear topside and remaining cautious during outdoor activities.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles daily briefing for March 19, 2026: Barbados reports 77°F with ENE winds at 16 knots under partly cloudy skies and a low 15% chance of rain. Expect persistent easterly trades across the region, with overcast conditions and light rain chances (15-23%) through Day 3. Winds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, but moderate 14-16 kt trades support stable sea states for most ports including St. Lucia, Martinique, and Antigua. Mariners should secure light gear topside and monitor localized squalls, especially near Dominica and St. Vincent where sea breezes may enhance shower activity. Fishing operations can proceed with caution; yacht captains are advised to maintain standard anchor watch routines in well-sheltered harbors like St. Maarten and Grenada.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and adjacent ports will see light southeast winds at 2 knots this morning under clear skies and dry conditions. Expect a warm day with afternoon highs near 85 degrees, but morning fog will reduce visibility around Los Angeles Harbor and Catalina Island’s Avalon anchorage. Mariners should exercise caution entering or departing these areas until fog lifts. Winds remain gentle through the next three days with occasional fog early followed by mostly overcast afternoons; no marine wind warnings are in effect. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for smooth seas but monitor fog advisories for safe navigation near Long Beach and Marina del Rey.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada and surrounding waters remain foggy this morning with light WNW winds near 1 knot and temperatures around 59°F. Expect persistent fog through the day with light WSW winds increasing to 6 knots and highs near 67°F; no rain is expected. Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos anchorages will have reduced visibility—exercise caution during navigation and maintain slow speeds. Fishing captains should prepare for improving visibility by day three as temperatures rise to mid-80s with overcast skies and light WSW winds near 7 knots; no marine warnings are in effect.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports will experience mild northwest winds of 11-15 knots through March 21 with stable, mostly clear to partly cloudy skies and no precipitation expected. Current conditions in Cabo show 71°F with NNW winds at 13 knots and calm seas, ideal for safe navigation and fishing operations. Yacht and fishing captains should note steady wind directions for anchorage planning, especially in La Paz where NW winds favor sheltered harbors. Cruise passengers can expect pleasant weather for shore excursions with comfortable temperatures and minimal wind impact.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and adjacent ports including Topolobampo and Altata will experience calm, stable conditions today with light NNW winds near 1 knot and clear skies. Winds will increase slightly over the next three days, shifting from WNW at 7 knots to S at 7 knots, accompanied by partly cloudy skies and zero precipitation chances. Mariners should expect smooth seas with no advisories in effect; yacht and fishing captains can plan for safe anchorage and fishing in protected bays without concern for sudden weather changes. Cruise passengers can enjoy comfortable, mild temperatures in the mid-70s Fahrenheit and clear to partly cloudy skies throughout the period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports will see mild conditions today with temperatures near 76°F, light NNE winds at 3 knots, and partly cloudy skies. Winds will shift west and increase slightly over the next three days, reaching up to 9 knots with overcast skies but no rain expected. Mariners should find calm seas suitable for tender operations and anchoring at Yelapa and Chacala, with no small craft advisories in effect. Fishing captains can anticipate stable conditions for offshore activities; however, keep an eye on the gradual wind increase from the west for maneuvering near Punta Mita channel entrances.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 19, 2026: Barcelona reports steady NE winds at 6 knots with overcast skies and no rain expected today. Winds will increase slightly from the ENE at 11 knots tomorrow with a 5% chance of light rain, followed by lighter NNE winds and clear skies on day two. By day three, expect a shift to SSW at 7 knots and partly cloudy conditions. Sea states remain moderate with no advisories; yacht captains should find safe conditions for anchorage at Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza. Cruise passengers should dress in layers and be prepared for cool, overcast weather with light winds; fishing captains can expect calm seas favorable for morning operations but monitor wind shifts by the third day.
regions.central-mediterranean:
Central Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 19, 2026:

Current conditions in Naples show mild temperatures near 51°F with light NNW winds at 4 knots under overcast skies and no precipitation expected today. Winds will increase from the NNE to 13 knots tomorrow with a 50% chance of rain, easing to 9-8 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain chances on days two and three. Mariners should anticipate moderate northerly winds especially near Naples, Civitavecchia, and Genoa; small craft operators should exercise caution in exposed anchorages such as Corsica and Sardinia where gusts may increase. Cruise passengers should prepare for intermittent showers tomorrow, while fishing vessels should monitor local rain chances and maintain readiness for reduced visibility and slick decks during periods of rainfall.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 19, 2026:

Current conditions in Athens (Piraeus) show 53°F with light northeast winds at 3 knots and overcast skies. Rain chances are low this morning but will increase to 65% later today with northerly winds strengthening to 8 knots and steady rain expected. Ports from Santorini to Rhodes should prepare for wet conditions and reduced visibility through Day 1. By Day 3, rains taper off with overcast skies and moderate northerly winds near 10 knots, easing navigation for yachts and fishing vessels. Mariners are advised to secure loose gear and monitor forecasts for localized showers, especially near coastal anchorage at Izmir and Haifa where gusts may briefly pick up. Passengers should plan indoor activities during peak rain periods and exercise caution on decks.
